title: Selenicereus nelsonii
taxonomy: Selenicereus nelsonii is classified within the Cactaceae family, which is the family of cacti. It belongs to the genus Selenicereus, a group of epiphytic cacti native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The species is a part of the tribe Hylocereeae, known for its climbing habits and funnel-shaped flowers. At the family level, Selenicereus nelsonii falls under the order Caryophyllales. This classification places Selenicereus nelsonii alongside other flowering plants that share similar characteristics.
